name: Rhode Island
tooltip: Rhode Island — Cat 2: Privacy-tort statute, no post-mortem term. Two 2025 deepfake statutes (NCII + election).
rightOfPublicity: Limited — privacy-tort posture. R.I. Gen. Laws § 9-1-28 (commercial misappropriation) and § 9-1-28.1 (Prosser privacy torts including appropriation). Covers name and image misappropriation.
aiCoverage: Yes — two 2025 enactments. 2025-H 5046 / S 0136 (deepfake-NCII, signed July 2, 2025) and 2025-H 5872 / S 0816 (election synthetic media disclosure, signed July 2025).
remedy: Civil suit under § 9-1-28 or § 9-1-28.1 for actual damages plus equitable relief. *Mendosa v. Time, Inc.*, 678 F. Supp. 967 (D.R.I. 1988) and *Leddy v. Narragansett Television L.P.*, 843 A.2d 481 (R.I. 2004) are the controlling cases.
whatsInteresting: Rhode Island has the same structural weakness as Massachusetts — privacy-tort statute, no descendibility, no post-mortem — but the 2025 legislature passed two AI-specific statutes back-to-back, which signals where the state is heading. For practical purposes, if you're a Rhode Island resident and your face shows up in AI-generated explicit content or a fake political ad, you have specific statutory hooks. For broader commercial misuse, you're in privacy-tort territory.
